title: Fill, Fallbacks, Scan Lines, Holds & Sticky Tables
description: Five fresh drops: scroll-driven text fill, Chrome-143 anchor-fallback-aware panels, CRT scan-line typography, hold-to-confirm buttons, and a sticky resizable data table.

drops.title: Scroll-Fill Text Reveal
drops.description: Headlines paint themselves in with color as they cross the viewport, driven purely by scroll position.
drops.concept: background-clip: text turns a linear/radial gradient into the fill for glyphs, and animation-timeline: view() ties that gradient's growth to how far the element has scrolled through the viewport instead of to clock time — a zero-JS scroll-scrubbed reveal that's become a signature 2026 hero-section technique.
drops.source: dev.to/pawar-shivam7 — "Scroll-Based Text Reveal Effect Using Pure CSS (No JS, No Motion Library)"

interaction.mouse: Scroll down to trigger each of the three reveals
interaction.click: Adjust the beam-width slider in the debug panel
interaction.touch: Scroll the page with a finger

drops.title: Anchor Fallback-Aware Panels
drops.description: Popovers flip to fit near viewport edges, and their internal layout reflows to match whichever fallback was actually chosen.
drops.concept: CSS anchor positioning's position-try-fallbacks already flips an overflowing popover automatically, but until Chrome 143 there was no way to know which fallback landed. container-type: anchored plus @container anchored(fallback: ...) closes that gap, letting a panel's icon side, arrow, and layout react to its own final placement — this demo ships that native path behind a feature check and a JS-measured equivalent so it works everywhere today.
drops.source: developer.chrome.com/blog — "Detect fallback positions with anchored container queries from Chrome 143"

interaction.mouse: Click each corner button to open its popover and watch it flip
interaction.click: Same as mouse — every trigger is a button
interaction.touch: Tap a corner button; near an edge it visibly flips and reflows

drops.title: Digital Scan Line Text
drops.description: A glowing beam sweeps through headline glyphs, plus a CRT-glitch and a chromatic-aberration variant.
drops.concept: A moving linear/radial gradient clipped to text via background-clip: text, animated with a wide background-size and background-position keyframe, reads as a scanning light beam painting the letters. Layering channel-offset clip-path pseudo-elements on top produces a stuttering signal-glitch read, all without touching individual characters in markup.
drops.source: veebilehed24.ee — "30 Modern CSS Text Effects (2026)" (Digital Scan Line Text)

interaction.mouse: Hover isn't required — the sweep loops continuously
interaction.click: Drag the beam-width and speed sliders
interaction.touch: Sliders work with touch drag too

drops.title: Hold-to-Confirm Radial Button
drops.description: Press-and-hold buttons whose ring fills over the action's real risk window instead of popping a confirmation dialog.
drops.concept: A stroke-dashoffset transition on an SVG ring is given a duration matching the hold requirement (3s for delete, 1.5s for archive, 2s for launch); pointerdown starts it, pointerup before completion snaps it back fast. It replaces an interruptive modal with friction proportional to the action's stakes — an increasingly common pattern for destructive actions in 2026 mobile-first UI.
drops.source: tomj.pro — "Why Holding Buttons Is Superior to Confirmation Dialogs in UX Design"

interaction.mouse: Press and hold each button until the ring completes
interaction.click: A quick click alone won't confirm anything

drops.title: Sticky Resizable Data Table
drops.description: A data table with a sticky header, sticky first column, drag-to-resize columns, and click-to-sort.
drops.concept: Combining position: sticky on both the thead cells and the first td/th of every row keeps row labels and column headers in view while scrolling in either direction — the classic 'frozen pane' spreadsheet UX built from two CSS declarations, layered here with pointer-driven column-width drag handles and click-to-sort headers.
drops.source: css-tricks.com — "A Table With Both a Sticky Header and a Sticky First Column"

interaction.mouse: Scroll the table, drag a header's resize handle, click a header to sort
interaction.click: Click any header to sort by that column
interaction.touch: Scroll and resize both work with touch drag
